programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Amanda's frightened friend does a disappearing act shortly after giving birth to quadruplets, leaving a flustered Jack Stewart holding the babies - and Dr Sloan investigating the activities of an illegal adoption ring. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A woman claims her rock-star husband was killed by an alien - which also impregnated her for good measure. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: The Fugitive

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry Mason defends a young girl who is accused of murdering her stepfather with a home-made bomb.

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: A gang of violent drug dealers come looking for a stolen bag of money at the weekend retreat where Walker is staying with a group of underprivileged teenagers

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Lindsay investigate the fatal shooting of singer Kid Rock's chauffeur, whose body has been found backstage by a fan. The case proves baffling, and as events unfold, a second murder takes place - in Stella's apartment. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise, Anna Belknap and Melina Kanakaredes

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A lieutenant apparently commits suicide by jumping from an aircraft carrier and Ziva and McGee are dispatched to inform the widow - but arrive to find that she has been killed. Subsequent inquiries reveal that the injuries on her body were inflicted after death and the murder scene was carefully staged to mislead investigators. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen and Sam investigate the mysterious death of a Marine in Afghanistan, urged on by the victim's distraught wife, who is adamant the Navy is hiding something from her. Meanwhile, the appearance of operational psychologist Nate Getz leads to fears that an evaluation is on the horizon. Chris O'Donnell, LL Cool J, Linda Hunt and Peter Cambor star

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble is caught in a survival struggle between a young racist condemned for murder and a sheriff with a cause

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A secretary who believes her boss is being blackmailed comes to see Perry Mason with a suitcase full of money

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An army scientist goes missing, so Abby is asked to temporarily replace him on a top-secret research project - but Gibbs later learns her assignment is connected to a current murder investigation, and worries she may meet the same fate as her predecessor. Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs goes into his favourite diner for a cup of coffee and finds himself staring down the barrel of a gun, forcing him to question choices he has made in the past and present. The action then flashes back a few days to when he and the rest of the team members were investigating the death of a petty officer on board USS Gentry, which has been undergoing repairs in dry dock. The 200th episode of the crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.desc: Callen goes undercover to infiltrate a network of Chechen terrorists, posing as a foreign fighter who wants to help them orchestrate their next attack. Chris O'Donnell, Daneila Ruah, Eric Christian Olsen and Barrett Foa star

programme.desc: Part one of two. With Donald Sinden and Jenny Seagrove

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Enforcement officers James and Mark help an 85-year-old man who is owed thousands by a roofing company who failed to pay the money the courts awarded

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A truck driver is diverted along a narrow road that cannot cope with the weight of his vehicle, with disastrous consequences. Paramedics treat an unresponsive baby

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp hosts as Ben Cooper and Tim Weeks help teams select items at the antiques fair at the Highland Centre in Edinburgh, in the hope of making a profit at auction. The host also discovers an interesting find at the fair that is not to everyone's taste

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al helps a local councillor try to reconnect with his estranged, grieving son. Medical drama, starring Ian Midlane

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz in which contestants aim to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest. Battling it out today are Shaun, Gina, John and Jane

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple search for a rural home in West Yorkshire with a budget of £500,000, which must have enough space to fit a beloved model railway. The presenter also visits one of the county's heritage fabric mills

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock takes on the Rich brothers to win the pitch for an elaborate Chelsea Flower Show-inspired makeover in Gloucester. The vast plot of land wraps around the house and boasts a brook and beautiful views, but it's an uncoordinated and untidy mess that embarrasses home-owners Sam and Sean. They have £5,000 to put things right but it's an ambitious project. Whose design will win the day?

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steven Moore and Natasha Raskin Sharp drive to the Scottish Borders, finding items including a tobacco barrel, an 18th-century teapot and a travel case with a secret compartment

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Nationwide reports and live studio-based chat, presented by Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents as more members of the public try to persuade the dealers to bid on their varied and unusual items. Shop owner Lindsay from Sunderland brings in a pair of optician's mirrors - one concave and the other convex - Lucinda has an item that has the dealers puzzled at first, until the owner reveals it is an antique ice shaver, and Sheffield man Dean hopes to make money from a vintage child's penny farthing

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metalwork expert Dominic Chinea is called in to saved a beloved garden bench that is on its last legs. For owner Margaret, this is where she first met her husband in his mother's garden, and later was the seat her father took his tea breaks on. Steve Fletcher gets to work on an old calculator which is completed jammed up, Suzie Fletcher gives some much-needed TLC to an antique bridle and Tim Gunn tries to get a rusty 1930s toy scooter rolling again

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett presents as the contestants create garments inspired by the movies, beginning with a pattern challenge to make Baby's iconic dress from Dirty Dancing. For the transformation round, the judges take their inspiration from Julie Andrews in The Sound of Music, asking the sewers to turn old curtains into children's clothes, while the made-to-measure task sees them creating dazzling 1970s-influenced disco dresses, as worn by the Dreamgirls

programme.title: Scam City: Money, Mayhem and Maseratis
programme.desc: Mobeen Azhar tries to understand what drives people to trade and uncovers a link between an established financial brand with offices in the City of London and the case of Gurvin

programme.title: Scam City: Money, Mayhem and Maseratis
programme.desc: Mobeen Azhar gets closer to Gurvin and a meeting with a whistleblower raises questions about marketing and responsibility in the multi-billion-pound world of retail forex trading

programme.title: What Are We Feeding Our Kids?
programme.desc: Across the world childhood obesity rates have risen tenfold in 50 years. In the UK 21 per cent of children are living with obesity when they leave primary school - the highest it's ever been. Ultra-processed food is cheap and convenient, and accounts for the majority of young people's calorie intake - 64 per cent for children and 67 per cent for teenagers. Yet alarmingly, scientists know very little about exactly how it affects youngsters' bodies. Dr Chris van Tulleken investigates the impact of ultra-processed food, as he undergoes a gruelling self-experiment that even shocks the scientists

programme.title: Joey Essex: Grief and Me
programme.desc: The reality TV star opens up about the death of his mother, who took her own life when he was 10 years old. With the help of clinical psychologist Dr Stephen Blumenthal, Joey sets out to finally face the impact his mum's death has had on him. Until now he's only opened up about his childhood with his family, but sister Frankie and cousin Chloe recognise that he's never truly processed his feelings or fully faced his bereavement

programme.desc: A trip through Scotland sees Natasha Raskin Sharp get to grips with haggis, while Steven Moore falls for an exquisite French lady

programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville creates a special blend of snuff, but will his customers notice any beneficial effects? The ladies get dressed up for line-dancing, while Gastric is prompted to go on a diet. A furtive Mr Newbold thinks he's found a pastime to share with Mrs Rossi - away from the prying eyes of Mrs Featherstone. However, a determined Black Widow enlists assistance from Granville and Leroy for a crash-course in how to ride a bicycle. David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben meets up with Callum only to find him with a colleague and quickly senses he is not welcome. Kheerat opens up to Sharon about Chantelle. Phil and Kat take Bert and Ernie to the school event, where they bump into Jack and Raymond. However, on the way home Kat finds herself in a frightening situation

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Steph McGovern, Richard Osman, Jennifer Saunders and Joe Sugg join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Aisling Bea is guest host this time around, with comedian Roisin Conaty and journalist Clive Myrie joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the news

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: A special regal edition sees a member of the royal family welcomed to the studio. But can Alan maintain his dignity in the face of a career-defining setback? Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu and Tim Key

programme.title: Gary Barlow: I'm with the Band
programme.desc: The Take That star brings together artists and musicians to perform classic songs from past and present. The band comprises Jamie Cullum (piano and vocals), James Bay (guitar and vocals), Mica Paris (vocals), Guy Chambers (keyboards and musical director), Tom Fletcher (guitar and vocals), Danya Fisher (bass) and Donavan Hepburn (drums), and of course Gary on keys and vocals. He also welcomes special guests to the show, as Anne-Marie and Craig David drop by and collaborate with Gary and the band, talk about their careers and musical heroes, and perform new versions of classic tracks

programme.title: A Fish Called Wanda (1988)
programme.desc: A stuffy English barrister representing a jewel thief is targeted by his client's partners in crime - a seductive American crook and her dim-witted would-be philosopher lover who will go to any lengths to discover where the diamonds from their last heist have been hidden. Crime comedy, starring John Cleese, Jamie Lee Curtis, Kevin Kline, Michael Palin and Tom Georgeson

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers take on the job of redesigning an unusually shaped garden in Droitwich Spa, whose owners want different areas for entertaining and relaxing

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a pair of early retirees find a new home in Wiltshire or Somerset - but it must be a period property with high ceilings and at least four bedrooms

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Yvonne and Julian find out about mental health in men and boys, and learn the importance of wild gardens and try out a running app. With voiceover

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Brian Cunningham reviews the success of Beechgrove's box-hedging alternatives, while he and Mairi Rattray also share topiary tips. Plus, catching up with the Seil Community Garden, which Beechgrove helped create in 2009.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc hosts the game show in which pairs of contestants go head to head attempting to crack a series of passwords, in a bid to win £2,500

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin hosts the antiques-buying challenge from the Midlands, where Emma Wilson from Teesside and David Broughton from Sussex go head to head going to auction and buy collectable items which they hope to sell on to maximize their profits

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North West chefs compete for a place in the national finals, preparing their entire menus for judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort, Andi Oliver and guest judge Greg Rusedski. Greg, a former British number one tennis player, understands the high standards expected at his sport's Grand Slam contests, in this Wimbledon-themed series

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The first of the North East heats sees returning champion Tommy Banks and newcomers Danny Parker and Josh Overington prepare their starters. Tommy draws inspiration from the traditional celebration of midsummer, Danny creates a dish using 22 vegetables, while Josh's centres on a savoury strawberry veloute

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan report live from Norfolk, and Iolo Williams heads up into the Scottish Highlands to spot some flamboyant avian show-offs. Meanwhile, Gillian Burke looks at some clever cuttlefish in Northern Ireland

programme.title: Big Blue UK
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and his team find out where sharks can be spotted in waters surrounding the UK, while Lindsay Chapman looks for shark egg cases washed up on our shore. Can she also find live shark embryos in amongst Devon's seaweed?

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the City Hall in Kingston upon Hull, antiques experts Caroline Hawley and David Harper hunt for objects to take to auction. Caroline is entertained by the story behind some Laurel and Hardy autographs and David is gripped by a collection of postcards between young lovers from the Edwardian era. Presenter Paul Martin talks to playwright James Graham about the legacy of Hull's year as the UK's City of Culture

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nish Kumar, Clara Amfo, Anneka Rice and Al Murray continue to test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches the Westerglen Transmitting Station in Falkirk, before visiting Gleneagles and Berry Town on route to his final stop on this leg, the city of Dundee

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v New Zealand. Action from day one of the First Test in the two-match series, held at Lord's. The sides last met in this format on English soil in 2015, when the hosts recorded a 124-run victory here, before the Black Caps ensured the series ended in a draw by prevailing by 199 runs at Headingley

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Insect fan Penny Metal introduces viewers to the tiny residents in a London park, and Iolo Williams checks out some alien species on the forest floor at Alladale Wilderness Reserve. Meanwhile, Gillian Burke looks at spring from a swift's point of view at WWT Castle Espie

programme.title: Building Britain's Biggest Nuclear Power Station
programme.desc: Following the challenges involved with building the Hinkley Point C in the remote Somerset countryside, the first new nuclear power station in Britain for a generation. This series follows the engineers, technicians, and the behind-the-scenes staff who are under pressure to keep the project on track, including building the mammoth foundations for the two nuclear reactors, and excavating cooling water tunnels out under the Bristol Channel

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Stand-up comedian Rob Beckett plays host in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience and then introduces routines by rising comedy stars Jen Brister and Darren Harriott

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: As the third lockdown begins to lift in the middle of April 2021, emergency cases inevitably begin to rise, which puts an incredible amount of pressure of bed availability and staff across the country. At Coventry's University Hospital, Critical Care is at full capacity, and two neurosurgeons must find a solution to treat both of their patients with only one bed available

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Yvonne and Julian find out about mental health in men and boys, and learn the importance of wild gardens and try out a running app. With voiceover

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Plant-able children's books are the offering from two friends hoping to gain a Dragon to grow their brand, and a pair of ex-bankers pitch their ambitious at-home private chef service. A Wirral-based entrepreneur pitches his range of luxury home fragrance products, while a husband-and-wife duo are keen to broaden out their scent-detection courses for dogs. Whether multimillionaires Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies will smell any potential profit remains to be seen. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: Mary Beard and guests discuss how we remember - not just as individuals, but as a society and a nation. She is joined by architect David Adjaye, writer Elif Shafak, artists Edmund de Waal and Cornelia Parker, poet Lemn Sissay, and actor Jane Horrocks

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels to leafy Hatch End in North West London, where he meets the great-nephew of the famous illustrator William Heath Robinson. Michael continues his journey through Slough and Stoke Poges before reaching Windsor, where he surveys the great walls of the castle, chief residence of the British monarch and the oldest and largest occupied castle in the world

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock takes on the Rich brothers to win the pitch for an elaborate Chelsea Flower Show-inspired makeover in Gloucester. The vast plot of land wraps around the house and boasts a brook and beautiful views, but it's an uncoordinated and untidy mess that embarrasses home-owners Sam and Sean. They have £5,000 to put things right but it's an ambitious project. Whose design will win the day?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple from Essex need Nicki Chapman's help to find a new home in Somerset. Nicki also visits a local farm to sample some homemade specialities made from an ancient grain

programme.title: All That Glitters: Britain's Next Jewellery Star
programme.desc: It's the grand final, and the three finalists return to the workshop to compete to be crowned the winner. They are each challenged to make two pieces of truly outstanding jewellery - pair of pearl earrings and a bridal headpiece. Who will the judges decide is worthy of the title Britain's next big jewellery star?

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc in which pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords as they attempt to take home the £2,500 prize money

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Romano Barca from Portsmouth and Jan Griffin from Essex go up against each other as Paul Martin shows them the tricks of the antiques trade before they face an auction showdown

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Josh Overington creates a fish stew made up of champagne, lobster, scallops and truffles, while Danny Parker creates a dish of scallop served with grapes and apple. And Tommy Banks uses turbot to create a meal inspired by the iconic Wimbledon food, strawberries and cream, but will which will impress the latest veteran chef?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The North East chefs go all out to impress with their main courses, as they compete for a chance to cook at the Taste of Summer banquet. Tommy Banks creates a dish inspired by Wimbledon's guard of honour, Danny Parker tries to recreate the flavours of an American barbecue, and Josh Overington presents a main course inspired by memories of his grandfather

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Insect fan Penny Metal introduces viewers to the tiny residents in a London park, and Iolo Williams checks out some alien species on the forest floor at Alladale Wilderness Reserve. Meanwhile, Gillian Burke looks at spring from a swift's point of view at WWT Castle Espie

programme.title: Big Blue UK
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and his team of experts turn their attention to seabirds, and Richard Taylor Jones aims to dispel a popular misconception about 'seagulls'

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Anita Manning value items brought to Blackburn Cathedral, while Paul Martin learns about how mill workers in the town used to be woken by a human alarm clock

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Comedians Al Murray and Nish Kumar, presenter Anneka Rice and Radio 1 DJ Clara Amfo continue the daily test of their general knowledge skills, hosted by Richard Osman

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his railway journey from Newcastle to Loch Ness, steered by his 1930s Bradshaw's Guide. Stopping at Dundee, Michael heads for Glamis Castle, where Lady Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon, the Queen Mother, grew up. Michael hears about her happy childhood and how she later found sanctuary there, when King Edward VIII abdicated and she and Prince Albert unexpectedly became king and queen of the United Kingdom

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v New Zealand. Action from day two of the First Test in the two-match series, held at Lord's. England have a 100 per cent record in Test matches against the Black Caps this Millennium, having the last five meetings here in this format

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Featuring an island paradise for puffins in Scotland and golden hares in Northern Ireland. Meanwhile, Chris Packham checks in on the live nests, and Michaela Strachan reports on the plight of the Norfolk plovers

programme.title: The Road to Partition
programme.desc: Part two of two. On June 22, 1921, King George V and Queen Mary arrived in Belfast for the official opening of the first parliament of Northern Ireland. The King's visit to Belfast was the culmination of three centuries of history. This concluding film examines how political tensions and outbreaks of sectarian violence threatened to destabilise the new Northern Irish state

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and series regular Hugh Dennis excavate the treasures of the week past with the help of guests Maisie Adam, Angela Barnes, Ed Byrne, Milton Jones and Kae Kurd

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: A filmic offshoot of Tim Key's stage show Megadate, in which a man dazzles a woman on the perfect first date. However, she doesn't text him back, leading him to retrace his steps. His path is riddled with cynics and naysayers, questioning his optimistic outlook on how the date went. The nerd in the bowling alley who thought he'd embarrassed himself, the old eel catcher on the mud flats trying to douse his dreams with wisdom, and the compassionate waitress at the chicken shop. Starring Tim Key, Mandeep Dhillon, Sam Fletcher, Bill Paterson and Julie Dray

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories. With Emma Barnett

programme.title: Building Britain's Biggest Nuclear Power Station
programme.desc: Following the challenges involved with building the Hinkley Point C in the remote Somerset countryside, the first new nuclear power station in Britain for a generation. This series follows the engineers, technicians, and the behind-the-scenes staff who are under pressure to keep the project on track, including building the mammoth foundations for the two nuclear reactors, and excavating cooling water tunnels out under the Bristol Channel

programme.title: Makeup: A Glamorous History
programme.desc: In the final episode, makeup artist Lisa Eldridge explores her favourite era for makeup, beauty and glamour, Britain in the 1920s. Lisa visits the British Film Institute archives to find out what impact the arrival of Hollywood cinema had on the development of some of our favourite makeup products, before working with pharmacist Szu Shen Wong to recreate authentic period recipes for foundation, eye makeup and lipstick

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewers take part in reduce, reuse and recycle week, in which all the fabric in the haberdashery is replaced with charity shop clothes and soft furnishings. They begin by following a pattern for a gentleman's waistcoat, followed by a transformation challenge in which they change items of army surplus into a stylish garment for a woman. Finally, they must create a made-to-measure dress using old jeans, before the sixth sewer is asked to leave. Judges Esme Young and Patrick Grant make that decision, while Joe Lycett presents

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo resumes his tour of the Home Counties in the historic town of Guildford. Later, in Chilworth, he follows his 1936 Bradshaw's guide to Newlands Corner on the slopes of the North Downs. Eventually he crosses into Hampshire to reach Aldershot

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Adrian and Gail dream of a contemporary garden to match their house in Holmfirth, but it must have an Australian twist. Charlie and the Rich Brothers try to achieve it for £5,000

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts finds a surprise hidden in a bungalow in Stafford and Dion Dublin heads to a semi-detached in Stoke-on-Trent. Plus, revisiting a former church in West Yorkshire, whose newlywed owners planned to transformed it into their dream forever home

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox is joined by comedian Rachel Parris, chef Rick Stein, actor and comedian Rob Delaney and presenter Vick Hope. They share favourite books as well as reviewing the week's two club picks - The Lying Life of Adults by Elena Ferrante, and Should We Fall Behind by Sharon Duggal

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads for Farnborough, Hampshire, famous today for its airshow and home to what was then the Royal Aircraft Establishment. In Basingstoke he visits a glorious neo-Gothic stately home set in the North Wessex Downs

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc in which pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords, in an attempt to take home £2,500 in cash

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin is in Shropshire with Sandra Zhang from Coventry and Ian Tully from Preston. They will be spending their hard earned cash in the hope they can make a profit

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs Tommy Banks, Josh Overington and Danny Parker continue the battle to cook at a Taste of Summer banquet celebrating 140 years of the Wimbledon Championships. As each of them prepare their desserts, they will all be out to impress as only two can make it through to the North East final

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs compete for a place in the national finals, preparing their entire menus for judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort, Andi Oliver and guest Judy Murray - mother of British number one tennis player Andy - as the programme continues its Wimbledon-themed series

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Featuring an island paradise for puffins in Scotland and golden hares in Northern Ireland. Meanwhile, Chris Packham checks in on the live nests, and Michaela Strachan reports on the plight of the Norfolk plovers

programme.title: Big Blue UK
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and his team focus on the array of weird and wonderful creatures on the UK's seashore, and Richard Taylor Jones goes in search of the barrel jellyfish

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Philip Serrell hunt for objects to take to auction at Blackburn Cathedral, Lancashire. Charles values a wooden Fijian club, while two vintage enamel signs catch Philip's eye. Presenter Paul Martin takes a look at the impressive Talbot collection - local press photographs taken of events, celebrities and the royal family over a 50-year period

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nish Kumar, Clara Amfo, Anneka Rice and Al Murray compete in Double Points Friday, and their scores are tallied across the week resulting in an overall champion being crowned

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is at Elgin's port, the coastal town of Lossiemouth, where the Labour Party's first prime minister James Ramsay MacDonald was born. Nearby, Michael finds a remote boarding school established in the 1930s and famous today both for its unusual ideas and its royal former pupils. His penultimate stop is Inverness, where he uncovers the work of female photographer MEM Donaldson, who documented a Highland way of life that was rapidly disappearing. Michael's journey ends at Loch Ness, where he joins a Deep Scan research team scouring the deep for signs of the elusive monster

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v New Zealand. Action from day three of the First Test in the two-match series, held at Lord's. The last Test series between the sides came in the winter of 2019, when New Zealand recorded a 1-0 series victory thanks to a comprehensive victory at Bay Oval and a draw at Seddon Park, but Joe Root's men will have been hopeful of making home advantage count on this occasion

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: There's theft in the Scottish seas - but do great skuas deserve their reputation as pirates of the skies? Megan McCubbin goes to the Welsh border to investigate why restoring peatland bog is so important for wildlife. There's also a live round-up of the wild soap operas playing out in Norfolk, and a peek at a secretive owl in Northern Ireland

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and Frances Tophill introduce a special Gardeners' World from Wakehurst, Kew's wild botanic garden in Sussex. They not only explore the gardens but also meet the gardeners, joining in on their planting projects and finding out more about the UK's largest conservation project, the Millennium Seed Bank

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Singer-songwriter Joan Armatrading chats to Jools and picks her favourite clips from the Later archive, including appearances by Tinie Tempah, the Ting Tings and Skepta. Rock band Field Music perform No Pressure at the Empire Theatre in their native Sunderland, and there is film of Los Angeles-based group Gabriels playing the title track from their debut EP Love and Hate in a Different Time

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's national and international news stories, with Mark Urban

programme.title: Zodiac (2007)
programme.desc: Fact-based crime thriller about the tortuous hunt for a mysterious serial killer who terrorised San Francisco in the late 1960s and early 1970s, and taunted the police and media with cryptic messages. Three men became obsessed with identifying the murderer after his reign of terror inexplicably stopped, but their fixation with the case took a heavy toll on their lives. Starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Robert Downey Jr, Mark Ruffalo and Brian Cox

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Anders undergoes brain surgery and his memory begins to return - revealing what happened to the 13th colony, how the five remaining Cylons survived and how their humanoid counterparts gained the ability to resurrect themselves. Meanwhile, it is discovered the Galactica's much-needed repairs require the use of Cylon technology - risking a rebellion if the decision were made to proceed

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Ellen Tigh returns to the Galactica, but her passionate reunion with Saul is short-lived when she learns of his relationship with Six and their expected child. The remaining Cylons are delighted by her return, however, and she begins to encourage them to leave and begin a new life away from the ship

programme.title: Saved By a Stranger
programme.desc: Identical twin brothers George and Peter who fled Nazi Germany in 1939 search for the family of the man who helped them escape. Plus, Clare, who housed 21 child refugees from wartorn Bosnia, looks for their mystery schoolgirl translator Petra, whose language skills allowed the children to properly communicate and share the horrors they had fled. Presented by Anita Rani

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A Pembrokeshire Farm
programme.desc: As summer arrives, the farmhouse is stripped down to its bare essentials, and Griff Rhys Jones meets geologist Sid Howells to explore what secrets the nearby rocks are holding

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross starts off with a partially blackened canvas as he creates a dark, mood-filled, misty waterfall scene

programme.title: Skies Above Britain
programme.desc: Racing champion Martin, who is a member of Britain's oldest flying club The Royal Aero Club, prepares to compete for the Schneider Trophy, in which 14 pilots will fly around outlying islands in Alderney in the Channel Islands. Elsewhere, a cargo pilot contends with a tight schedule, and must cross the Irish sea even during bad weather conditions, while the team at DHL's warehouse processes a hundred and fifty thousand parcels each night. Plus, at NATS, bad weather grounds planes and dangerous 'wind sheer' causes pilots to abort landings

programme.title: Blitz: The Bombs That Changed Britain
programme.desc: The documentary investigates the deadly impact of an incendiary bomb that set fire to St Peter's Church in the historic heart of Bristol on the night of Sunday November 24, 1940. Almost 80 years later, Geoffrey Serle, who had been attending church with his father when the raid began, recounts their desperate attempt to find shelter amongst the chaos. The film also uses accounts from the time, recalling how Bristol's firefighters simply could not cope

programme.title: How to Build
programme.desc: Documentary following workers at Rolls-Royce's Derby factory as they construct the company's Trent 1000 jet engine in preparation for its maiden flight attached to a Boeing 787 Dreamliner. The film explores the technology involved in its construction, and charts the ups and downs of working on the assembly line. Narrated by Gerard Fletcher

programme.title: Great British Photography Challenge
programme.desc: The photographers use their smartphones to capture fast-moving action in a boxing arena, and are set further challenges by artist Maryam Wahid and celebrity stylist Cheryl Konteh

programme.title: Blinded: Those Who Kill
programme.desc: Louise gets far too close to Peter. unaware of the terrible truth about him lurking beneath the surface. Karina tries to find new evidence for the investigation. In Danish

programme.title: Blinded: Those Who Kill
programme.desc: The police take Johannes to the station, and Louise fears that the truth about her and Peter Vinge will be revealed. Peter meets his ex-wife. In Danish

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross starts off with a partially blackened canvas as he creates a dark, mood-filled, misty waterfall scene

programme.title: A Pembrokeshire Farm
programme.desc: As summer arrives, the farmhouse is stripped down to its bare essentials, and Griff Rhys Jones meets geologist Sid Howells to explore what secrets the nearby rocks are holding

programme.title: Ian Hislop's Olden Days
programme.desc: The satirist examines Britain during the Industrial Revolution and Victorian era, when writers, artists and politicians tried to make sense of the changing world by seeking inspiration from the Middle Ages. Novelist Walter Scott's tales of gallant heroes and knights in armour in his books Waverley and Ivanhoe brought him huge fame and success, while dissatisfied workers formed an ancient-style band of brothers to protest against industrialisation, calling themselves the Luddites. The Houses of Parliament were rebuilt in a medieval gothic style and Prime Minister Benjamin Disraeli led a government that improved living and working conditions for millions by harking back to the values of the past

programme.title: A Pembrokeshire Farm
programme.desc: Building work moves to the inside of Griff Rhys Jones' house, and things are at last being built up instead of knocked down, but as the deadline approaches, tempers begin to fray

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints an icy pond and weather-worn cabin that appear to float in the air

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on the female stars of the Hollywood studio era, from its beginnings around 1910 through to its collapse in the early 1960s. Screen icons chronologically recalled include Theda Bara, Lillian Gish, Clara Bow, Louise Brooks, Marlene Dietrich, Greta Garbo, Joan Crawford, Bette Davis, Rita Hayworth, Ava Gardner, Elizabeth Taylor and Marilyn Monroe

programme.title: My Week with Marilyn (2011)
programme.desc: A film student gets a job as a production assistant on the set of movie The Prince and the Showgirl. He bears witness to the clash of conflicting personalities between leads Marilyn Monroe and Laurence Olivier, and becomes a confidant of the troubled, lonely Hollywood actress. Fact-based drama, starring Michelle Williams, Eddie Redmayne, Kenneth Branagh, Judi Dench and Emma Watson

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Profiles of handsome heroes and pin-ups of the silver screen, including Paul Newman, Robert Redford, Jane Russell and Marilyn Monroe. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: On Camera: Photographers at the BBC
programme.desc: The working practices, lives and opinions of some of the greatest photographers of the last 60 years, drawn from the BBC's archives. The programme brings into focus the key genres - fashion, portraiture, documentary and landscape - and the characters behind the camera who have helped defined them, including Norman Parkinson, David Bailey, Eve Arnold and Martin Parr

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints an icy pond and weather-worn cabin that appear to float in the air

programme.title: A Pembrokeshire Farm
programme.desc: Building work moves to the inside of Griff Rhys Jones' house, and things are at last being built up instead of knocked down, but as the deadline approaches, tempers begin to fray

programme.title: The Beauty of Anatomy
programme.desc: Dr Adam Rutherford tells the story of how Dr Henry Gray and Dr Henry Carter produced academic textbook Gray's Anatomy - the world's most famous study of the human body. Based on dissections they performed themselves, the accuracy of its descriptions and the stark beauty of its illustrations made it an instant bestseller

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on the female stars of the Hollywood studio era, from its beginnings around 1910 through to its collapse in the early 1960s. Screen icons chronologically recalled include Theda Bara, Lillian Gish, Clara Bow, Louise Brooks, Marlene Dietrich, Greta Garbo, Joan Crawford, Bette Davis, Rita Hayworth, Ava Gardner, Elizabeth Taylor and Marilyn Monroe

programme.title: Extra Life: A Short History of Living Longer
programme.desc: Steven Johnson and David Olusoga consider how data is the first line of defence against a new pandemic, before a vaccine or an effective treatment can come into play

programme.title: Pop Go the Sixties
programme.desc: Procol Harum's 1967 Top of the Pops performance of A Whiter Shade of Pale

programme.title: Singer-Songwriters at the BBC
programme.desc: An hour-long compilation of classic singles highlighting the growing popularity of the singer-songwriter genre during the 1970s, which became one of the decade's defining styles. Featuring music by Elton John, James Taylor, Cat Stevens, Harry Nilsson, Neil Diamond, Steve Goodman, Joni Mitchell and Neil Young

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1991
programme.desc: Anthea Turner presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 31 January 1991 and featuring EMF, Nomad and Soho

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1991
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 7 February 1991 and featuring the KLF, Kenny Thomas and Kim Appleby

programme.title: Once Were Brothers: Robbie Robertson and the Band (2019)
programme.desc: The story of how the Canadian guitarist overcame adversity and found camaraderie alongside the four other men who would become his brothers in music in the Band. The film blends rare archival footage, photography, songs and interviews with Robertson's friends and collaborators, including Martin Scorsese, Bruce Springsteen, Eric Clapton and Van Morrison

programme.title: Guitar, Drum and Bass
programme.desc: The American guitarist recounts the story of the quest for new guitar sounds, which has driven pop music from Les Paul's first guitar to Bo Diddley's tremolo, Duane Eddy's whammy bar, Keith Richards' fuzz pedal, the Who's feedback, the Byrds' 12 string, Hendrix's wah wah pedal, Uli Roth and Van Halen's shredding, the Edge's digital delay, Ry Cooder's slide and KT Tunstall and Ed Sheeran's looper pedals. Contributors include Duane Eddy, Roger McGuinn, the Edge, Bonnie Raitt, Seasick Steve, KT Tunstall, Joe Bonamassa, Uli Jon Roth, Vernon Reid, Heart's Nancy Wilson, the Runaways' Lita Ford and producer Shel Talmy

programme.title: Huey Morgan's Latin Music Adventure
programme.desc: The 6Music presenter embarks on a quest to understand Latin Music's enduring appeal. Arriving in Rio, Huey learns that Brazil's newly elected government has come out against the hedonistic atmosphere of carnival and that Mangueira Samba School group is just one of many planning a protest. Later, he meets Gilberto Gil, who pioneered a new, politically conscious sound known as Tropicália. It was so radical - and its social implications so profound - that Gil was imprisoned and finally exiled in 1969. In Salvador, Huey meets percussionist Carlinhos Brown to talk about how afro positive music has shaped the sound of Brazil

programme.title: Latin Music: A Session With
programme.desc: Bossacucanova is a Brazilian musical group combining traditional Bossa Nova with electronica. Pioneer of bossa nova Roberto Menescal, joins the band - which co-comprises his son Márcio - in their Rio studio, for a re-interpreting of classic Bossa hits. With contributions by Mustafa Baba Aussa and Marcio Menescal. Partly in Portuguese with English subtitles

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Erin Ranney is a wildlife camera operator and adventurer. She splits her time between Alaska and the Falklands and loves filming the planet’s most incredible wildlife. Erin’s mission is to film rockhopper penguins on the Falkland Islands and find out why these charismatic creatures are facing serious threats to their survival. She investigates why the rockhopper population has fallen by 80 per cent since the 1930s. Climate change is the biggest threat to the rockhoppers, causing violent storms that could see them penguins face extinction. Erin also finds out how planting tussac grass can combat climate change and discovers that what we do in our own countries can affect these penguins thousands of miles away. She talks about how we can all make a difference towards helping the planet, just by taking small steps

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Jahawi is an underwater camera operator who is passionate about preserving the coastline of Kenya, his home country. The ocean is home to a whole host of marine wildlife, but one species here is particularly under threat: dolphins. This fun and curious mammal is facing a number of issues, as their habitat is being destroyed and the fish they eat are being depleted. There is also significant human activity on this coastline, which is damaging the marine life. However, Lamu has stood the test of time and is a marine sanctuary for them, with a new port being built and things about to change. Working with the Kenyan Marine Mammal Network, Jahawi is hoping to get the area protected as a marine reserve before it’s too late. It doesn’t take long for Jahawi to find the resident bottle-nosed dolphins as they enjoy swimming at the bow of the boat, and he soon gets into the water with the dolphins to get a closer look at these magnificent creatures. here is one species of dolphin he is hoping to

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie visits a robot workshop where they design farming robots that help farmers grow crops. She meets three farming robots, Tom, Wilma and Richard that work together on the farm to help find the weeds in the fields. Maddie learns that Tom, a scanning robot, can see things we cannot see, using special cameras to scan and take photos of the weeds, animals, insects and crops in the field. Maddie then discovers how Tom sends pictures and information to an artificial intelligence computer called Wilma to build a map of the field. Finally, Maddie meets another Richard, a robot who uses the map to find and destroy the weeds. Maddie then visits a printing workshop to see how a 3D printer makes things. At the workshop, they design glasses frames to wear and Maddie uses her special camera to see how the 3D printing works by building up layers stacked on top of each other.

With Round 1 over, Arthur decides to put the trophies back in the lost-and-found box, where he got them. He’s amazed at the stuff that people leave lying around, like a rubber duck, a lamb teddy, and a mysterious and complicated-looking machine. Gail and Muddles are fascinated by the device. programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The bunny accidentally spills Krispos all over the kitchen floor when driving his car around the breakfast table - so tries to clean them up with a 'voo-voo cleaner'

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee is having a lovely cup of tea in his favourite cup when Roly surprises him with a very loud 'Peek-a-Boo', which is so loud that Duggee jumps and drops his cup which shatters into pieces

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: A big box is delivered to the Clubhouse, full of bits and pieces. Duggee and the squirrels are going to have to work very hard to put it all together

programme.title: Go Jetters

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘robot’, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see a red robot, a smiling robot, a building robot, a sleeping robot and a helping robot! Dee meets a bleeping robot and has a boogie with a group of dancing robots! The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee’s friend finally succeeds in saying today’s word!

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog and Pug meet Little Red Riding Hood and the Big Bad Wolf.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The friends play on a new climbing frame, but Bing waits and watches Padno go down a slide because he is too scared to try

programme.title: Twirlywoos

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Educational programme for children aged between four and seven

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The friends have fun moving backwards and forwards. The Tubby Custard Ride goes forwards, then backwards, forwards again!

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome little ones Minnie, Luna, Oliver, River, Theo and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their babies at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Nigel’s bag are a toy pig, a toy sheep and a toy chicken, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that are themed around things you might find at a farm. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate farm objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The fun song Old MacDonald Had A Farm features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring farm things too. The Together Song gets everyone moving about before some calm time with sensory scarves and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: When a dancing bug lands on Duggee's head, he can't stop dancing. The squirrels take a close look and see that it's a tiny hillbilly bug with a fiddle. What is more, he has got a whole band with him - the Hillbilly Bug Band

programme.title: My Petsaurus
programme.desc: Megan's perfected her bubble blowing mixture, but can she stop Fluffy popping them all?

programme.title: Kiri & Lou
programme.desc: Lou sings a song to conquer his fear, but wakes a sleeping dinosaur.

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: The Snowy Blower lives underground and loves blowing fountains of snow up into the air! B.O.T. must keep clear of the snowy eruptions and be quick to complete this mission.

programme.title: Andy's Baby Animals
programme.desc: Andy Day reveals the different ways in which animals sleep, revealing how many young apes must learn how to make a bed out of leaves. In Tibet, bar-headed geese make a soft fluffy nest out of feathers on the hard rock, while Meerkats spend the night in burrows underground

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Looking at iconic songs that represent the varied music of Black artists from Nina Simone to Bob Marley at various locations around the UK with links to Black history. Semhar and Tianah are at Bob Marley's London to look at his classic hit Three Little Birds

programme.desc: Explore a mosaic of a butterfly and a colour-changing robot, and watch as Book Girl makes a bridge

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join 13-month-old Gia and twins, Chester and Elias, as they discover the world around them and find out what words they are learning. Both families are playing at home, talking about colours and a four-legged friend. They all head to baby classes, where they use music to practice their words, and some puppets come along to help them learn! Back at home, Chester and Elias play in the garden with their brother, going up and down the slide. Gia takes mum and granddad for a walk in the woods, where they see lots of beautiful things - they even find a bird! Finally, Chester and Elias play a counting game and Gia makes a wonderful word wall in the garden using pictures of things they saw that day. Remember, every child develops differently.

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: Timmy and Tig visit a castle, and are afraid of some strange sounds until Woolly explains they are echoes and are nothing to be afraid of

programme.desc: The Squirrels are curious about Duggee's prized train set, but struggle to understand that his miniature locomotives are not really toys

programme.desc: YolanDa Brown and her house band The Band Jaminals entertain an audience of children

programme.desc: Al helps a local councillor try to reconnect with his estranged, grieving son. Medical drama, starring Ian Midlane

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock takes on the Rich brothers to win the pitch for an elaborate Chelsea Flower Show-inspired makeover in Gloucester. The vast plot of land wraps around the house and boasts a brook and beautiful views, but it's an uncoordinated and untidy mess that embarrasses home-owners Sam and Sean. They have £5,000 to put things right but it's an ambitious project. Whose design will win the day?

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steven Moore and Natasha Raskin Sharp drive to the Scottish Borders, finding items including a tobacco barrel, an 18th-century teapot and a travel case with a secret compartment

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents as more members of the public try to persuade the dealers to bid on their varied and unusual items. Shop owner Lindsay from Sunderland brings in a pair of optician's mirrors - one concave and the other convex - Lucinda has an item that has the dealers puzzled at first, until the owner reveals it is an antique ice shaver, and Sheffield man Dean hopes to make money from a vintage child's penny farthing

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metalwork expert Dominic Chinea is called in to saved a beloved garden bench that is on its last legs. For owner Margaret, this is where she first met her husband in his mother's garden, and later was the seat her father took his tea breaks on. Steve Fletcher gets to work on an old calculator which is completed jammed up, Suzie Fletcher gives some much-needed TLC to an antique bridle and Tim Gunn tries to get a rusty 1930s toy scooter rolling again

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett presents as the contestants create garments inspired by the movies, beginning with a pattern challenge to make Baby's iconic dress from Dirty Dancing. For the transformation round, the judges take their inspiration from Julie Andrews in The Sound of Music, asking the sewers to turn old curtains into children's clothes, while the made-to-measure task sees them creating dazzling 1970s-influenced disco dresses, as worn by the Dreamgirls

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Bethan Rhys Roberts presents the weekly show featuring stories and in-depth interviews about what is happening in Wales and why it matters

programme.title: Scam City: Money, Mayhem and Maseratis
programme.desc: Mobeen Azhar tries to understand what drives people to trade and uncovers a link between an established financial brand with offices in the City of London and the case of Gurvin

programme.title: Scam City: Money, Mayhem and Maseratis
programme.desc: Mobeen Azhar gets closer to Gurvin and a meeting with a whistleblower raises questions about marketing and responsibility in the multi-billion-pound world of retail forex trading

programme.title: What Are We Feeding Our Kids?
programme.desc: Across the world childhood obesity rates have risen tenfold in 50 years. In the UK 21 per cent of children are living with obesity when they leave primary school - the highest it's ever been. Ultra-processed food is cheap and convenient, and accounts for the majority of young people's calorie intake - 64 per cent for children and 67 per cent for teenagers. Yet alarmingly, scientists know very little about exactly how it affects youngsters' bodies. Dr Chris van Tulleken investigates the impact of ultra-processed food, as he undergoes a gruelling self-experiment that even shocks the scientists

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Emma reacts strangely to some good news, Norma's patience begins to wear thin and Sheriff Romero finally decides where his loyalties lie

programme.desc: Theo Paphitis makes a temporary comeback in the Den, standing in for Peter Jones who is self-isolating. The Dragons are bewildered by a multitudinous mix of merchandise on offer by a planet-saving entrepreneur, and finally an Irish couple pitch their anti-roll changing mat for babies

programme.title: Joey Essex: Grief and Me
programme.desc: The reality TV star opens up about the death of his mother, who took her own life when he was 10 years old. With the help of clinical psychologist Dr Stephen Blumenthal, Joey sets out to finally face the impact his mum's death has had on him. Until now he's only opened up about his childhood with his family, but sister Frankie and cousin Chloe recognise that he's never truly processed his feelings or fully faced his bereavement

He also visits the village of Fairbourne, and takes a ride on its special small-scale train. Archaeologist Dr Erin Lloyd-Jones goes in search of local gold and Matt Bassett goes stargazing, but is defeated by the Welsh weather

programme.desc: Ben meets up with Callum only to find him with a colleague and quickly senses he is not welcome. Kheerat opens up to Sharon about Chantelle. programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: Lockdown is finally lifted, and in Bridgend Scott gets to open his doughnuts and coffee cafe. Cardiff nightclub owner Panikos auditions Russian cook Shev for the role of head chef, while vintage clothing entrepreneur and keyboard player Nanial prepares for his band to launch their first album

programme.title: Gary Barlow: I'm with the Band
programme.desc: The Take That star brings together artists and musicians to perform classic songs from past and present. The band comprises Jamie Cullum (piano and vocals), James Bay (guitar and vocals), Mica Paris (vocals), Guy Chambers (keyboards and musical director), Tom Fletcher (guitar and vocals), Danya Fisher (bass) and Donavan Hepburn (drums), and of course Gary on keys and vocals. He also welcomes special guests to the show, as Anne-Marie and Craig David drop by and collaborate with Gary and the band, talk about their careers and musical heroes, and perform new versions of classic tracks

programme.title: A Fish Called Wanda (1988)
programme.desc: A stuffy English barrister representing a jewel thief is targeted by his client's partners in crime - a seductive American crook and her dim-witted would-be philosopher lover who will go to any lengths to discover where the diamonds from their last heist have been hidden. Crime comedy, starring John Cleese, Jamie Lee Curtis, Kevin Kline, Michael Palin and Tom Georgeson

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metalwork expert Dominic Chinea is called in to saved a beloved garden bench that is on its last legs. For owner Margaret, this is where she first met her husband in his mother's garden, and later was the seat her father took his tea breaks on. Steve Fletcher gets to work on an old calculator which is completed jammed up, Suzie Fletcher gives some much-needed TLC to an antique bridle and Tim Gunn tries to get a rusty 1930s toy scooter rolling again

programme.desc: Joe Lycett presents as the contestants create garments inspired by the movies, beginning with a pattern challenge to make Baby's iconic dress from Dirty Dancing. programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Theo Paphitis makes a temporary comeback in the Den, standing in for Peter Jones who is self-isolating. A father-and-daughter duo demonstrate their airbag safety equipment, and a self-balancing serving tray is put through its paces by a hands-on Theo. The Dragons are bewildered by a multitudinous mix of merchandise on offer by a planet-saving entrepreneur, and finally an Irish couple pitch their anti-roll changing mat for babies

programme.title: Joey Essex: Grief and Me
programme.desc: The reality TV star opens up about the death of his mother, who took her own life when he was 10 years old. With the help of clinical psychologist Dr Stephen Blumenthal, Joey sets out to finally face the impact his mum's death has had on him. Until now he's only opened up about his childhood with his family, but sister Frankie and cousin Chloe recognise that he's never truly processed his feelings or fully faced his bereavement

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: In a special episode, Dougie and team meet the young people who will steer the future of our countryside - the young farmers, crofters, conservationists and rural craftspeople. Anne visits a young couple who have just taken on a farm in Galloway, Dougie is in the north west with a dynamic woman who won 'Young Crofter of the year' and Euan finds out how young folk can be encouraged to stay in the countryside

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben meets up with Callum only to find him with a colleague and quickly senses he is not welcome. Kheerat opens up to Sharon about Chantelle. Phil and Kat take Bert and Ernie to the school event, where they bump into Jack and Raymond. However, on the way home Kat finds herself in a frightening situation

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville creates a special blend of snuff, but will his customers notice any beneficial effects? The ladies get dressed up for line-dancing, while Gastric is prompted to go on a diet. A furtive Mr Newbold thinks he's found a pastime to share with Mrs Rossi - away from the prying eyes of Mrs Featherstone. However, a determined Black Widow enlists assistance from Granville and Leroy for a crash-course in how to ride a bicycle. David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Aisling Bea is guest host this time around, with comedian Roisin Conaty and journalist Clive Myrie joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the news

programme.title: Gary Barlow: I'm with the Band
programme.desc: The Take That star brings together artists and musicians to perform classic songs from past and present. The band comprises Jamie Cullum (piano and vocals), James Bay (guitar and vocals), Mica Paris (vocals), Guy Chambers (keyboards and musical director), Tom Fletcher (guitar and vocals), Danya Fisher (bass) and Donavan Hepburn (drums), and of course Gary on keys and vocals. He also welcomes special guests to the show, as Anne-Marie and Craig David drop by and collaborate with Gary and the band, talk about their careers and musical heroes, and perform new versions of classic tracks

programme.title: A Fish Called Wanda (1988)
programme.desc: A stuffy English barrister representing a jewel thief is targeted by his client's partners in crime - a seductive American crook and her dim-witted would-be philosopher lover who will go to any lengths to discover where the diamonds from their last heist have been hidden. Crime comedy, starring John Cleese, Jamie Lee Curtis, Kevin Kline, Michael Palin and Tom Georgeson

programme.title: The Road to Partition
programme.desc: Part two of two. On June 22, 1921, King George V and Queen Mary arrived in Belfast for the official opening of the first parliament of Northern Ireland. The King's visit to Belfast was the culmination of three centuries of history. This concluding film examines how political tensions and outbreaks of sectarian violence threatened to destabilise the new Northern Irish state

programme.desc: Under cover of Covid, the Amazon rainforest is under attack. Deforestation is at levels not seen for more than a decade. Brazilian President, Jair Bolsonaro, talks of opening up the forest to development while the environmental police is under attack from loggers. Our World has obtained a recording of the Environment minister talking about using the cover of Covid to 'change all the rules' in the Amazon. For Our World, Justin Rowlatt, is on a mission to find out how a tribe he visited a decade ago is faring the face of this assault

programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: England v Austria (Kick-off 8.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents all the action from the friendly at Riverside Stadium in Middlesbrough, as the teams continue preparations for Euro 2020. This match and Sunday's clash with Romania will be the final opportunities for the England players to impress Gareth Southgate ahead of their opener against Croatia on June 13, as the nations meet for the first time since 2007, when Peter Crouch scored the only goal of the game in Vienna. With analysis from Roy Keane, Ian Wright and Eni Aluko, and commentary by Sam Matterface and Lee Dixon

programme.title: The Fast and the Furious (2001)
programme.desc: In a move to identify suspected hijackers, an undercover police officer infiltrates the high-octane world of illegal late-night street racing by rival gangs. He is befriended by a champion driver, but when he also forms a bond with the racer's sister, he is pitched into a conflict of loyalties. Thriller, starring Vin Diesel, Paul Walker, Michelle Rodriguez and Jordana Brewster

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois agrees to be a surrogate mother for a friend - who promptly dies unexpectedly, leaving her facing a dilemma surrounding the future of the unborn child

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie auditions in drag for his favourite TV show after discovering it is only searching for girls, and soon becomes a hit with viewers. Meg shocks the entire city by dating a conventionally attractive boy

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan has his plans for Thanksgiving ruined when Francine's adoptive parents pay them an unexpected visit. Meanwhile, Francine's mother tries to get Hayley back into the dating world by setting her up with an eligible Chinese bachelor, who reveals his true intentions at the dinner table. Featuring the guest voice of Mariah Carey

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Klaus discovers his original human body, but when Stan refuses to help him switch back, the fish comes up with a fitting form of revenge. Animated comedy about a man who works for the CIA and is constantly on the alert for terrorist activity

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Part one of two. Motivated by the promise of a new beachside burger joint, Bob conspires with Felix to persuade Mr Fischoeder to sell Wonder Wharf

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Part two of two. Felix puts Bob in danger when he resorts to desperate measures and Linda and the kids must come to the rescue

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Amy organises a community service event, but it's jeopardized by Jeff's return and Glenn's desire to help his co-workers. Sandra confronts Jonah about his strategy for soliciting donations, whileGarrett tries to prove his intelligence to Dina

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: Matt Edmondson reveals the cat with the world's longest fur, as well as attempts to spin three hula hoops on high-heeled roller skates and to push a car with a spear to the throat

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: ITV2 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alec tries to sign Sandy up for Golden Years. Roy and Hayley enjoy themselves sightseeing in Amsterdam. Maxine has no luck raising the deposit for the flat

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Hayley is sad that Roy has to return to England. Sandy starts doing some escort work for Alec. Emily organises a campaign photo shoot for Spider

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris finds his tyres slashed. Mandy is released from jail. Kelly demands that Scott leaves her alone. Kathy and Alice receive a surprise visitor

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kelly and Mandy discover Scott has crashed his car. Angie questions Chris about any enemies he might have. Elsa is keen to spend some time with Alice

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick investigates the theft of thousands of pounds' worth of wine from a warehouse. Meanwhile, trustworthy Greengrass becomes a gamekeeper and the truth behind Alf's strange behaviour is revealed. Rural police drama set in the rustic backwater of Aidensfield, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Tongues are set wagging when the house on the hill changes hands

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin comes home with a puppy, to Chrissy's dismay. Flat-share comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan, Sally Thomsett and Paula Wilcox

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As Foyle and his team search for a missing boy, their investigation leads them to a less than inviting hotel - and the discovery of the youngster's body in the nearby woods suggests one of the residents could be responsible. Milner finds his loyalty divided when Foyle is placed under house arrest, but the determined investigator vows to prove his innocence. Drama series, starring Michael Kitchen

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Susan is injured in a hit-and-run accident, Greengrass tries his hand at taxidermy, and Nick decides to study for his sergeant's exams. Rural police drama set in the rustic backwater of Aidensfield, starring Nick Berry

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris hires Terry to be his bodyguard. Bernice is nervous about the opening. Marlon flirts with Bernice in the hope of getting a full-time chef's job at the pub

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Claudia tells Zoe that Chris has not come home. Kathy tells Elsa to leave her and Alice alone. Angie hears that Chris's car has been found abandoned on waste ground in Hotten

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Hayley is thrilled when Roy invites her to stay the night at his flat. Michael teaches Jim how to dress himself. Spider hates seeing his tidied-up image on posters

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly is smitten by Lorraine but she is more interested in Spider. Maxine tells Greg she is in love with him. Roy takes Hayley boating and suggests they start an affair

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Japp and Poirot investigate the murder of an actress's estranged husband, only to find in the theatrical world, where deception and play-acting are a way of life, nothing is quite what it seems. In time-honoured fashion, it initially looks as though the butler did it - but subsequent inquiries throw up a veritable rogues' gallery of equally likely suspects. Philip Jackson, David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: DCI Stanhope and her team, including new recruit DC Hicham Cherradi, travel to a remote country house when the woman looking after it is killed. No sooner have they arrived, than a second body is discovered. Vera discovers the two victims met the night before their deaths, and goes in search of a connection between them. Forensics evidence reveals that one was definitely present when the other died - but when no murder weapon or motive can be found, Vera begins to suspect a third party was involved

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When a man's charred remains are found after an explosion, Foyle's investigations lead him to a top secret intelligence organisation. With murder and deception rife among its members, the race is on to find out the truth, but the detective's discoveries threaten his future career. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen with guests Angela Thorne, Ronald Pickup and Samuel West

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: After receiving a chilling phone call, the detective and sidekick DC Presley stumble on an exotic animal smuggling ring at a farm - where it appears an old adversary of Frost has been eaten by a crocodile. Meanwhile, a couple return from holiday to find the naked corpse of a man in their bedroom, prompting the husband to assume his wife was having an affair. Starring David Jason

programme.desc: Tongues are set wagging when the house on the hill changes hands
